abstract: A stellar four-women cast and a highly satisfying script make this story about a necklace changing hands pleasantly surprising.

headline.main: A Necklace as a Token of Ecstasy
headline.print_headline: A Necklace as a Token of Ecstasy

snippet: A stellar four-women cast and a highly satisfying script make this story about a necklace changing hands pleasantly surprising.

abstract: It has been done before. The movies have played the one-object-through-the-years- if-only-it-could-talk game with a yellow Rolls-Royce and a cursed red violin, among other things, with less than brilliant results. So you might approach the Primary Stages production of Michele Lowe's ''String of Pearls'' with lowered expectations. But you would be pleasantly surprised.     First, there's the stellar four-woman cast, which includes Ellen McLaughlin, the Tony Award-winning angel from ''Angels in America,'' and Mary Testa, the Tony-nominated alcoholic voice teacher from ''On the Town.'' They are joined by Antoinette LaVecchia and Sharon Washington, who have impressive skills of their own. Then there's Ms. Lowe's script which is a highly satisfying, often hilarious blend of sex, satire, poignancy and absurdism, with an emphasis on women's relationships and the inevitability of both loss and happiness. And it's directed with knowing sparkle by Eric Simonson.

headline.main: A Necklace as a Token of Ecstasy
headline.print_headline: A Necklace as a Token of Ecstasy

abstract: Participation of women in Chechen suicide terror attacks--despite culture's deeply patriarchal society--reflects radicalization of war that began as separatist struggle but has turned increasingly nihilistic; it also exposes deep schisms that are tearing apart Chechnya, where few people speak warmly of Russia or Kremlin, but where all express horror at recent attacks carried out for sake of Chechnya's independence, including bombing of two planes and siege at school; Chechens have not embraced cult of religious martyrdom, and their religious leaders do not preach fiery sermons revering them; those who knew four women involved in recent attacks say they cannot believe they were involved and speculate about some other fate that has befallen them; female suicide bombers have taken part in at least 15 attacks since war erupted again in 1999; some say decade of war and destruction, including atrocities by Russian forces, have driven women to desperate acts; photos; brief descriptions of some suicide bombings by Chechen women (L)

headline.main: From Dismal Chechnya, Women Turn to Bombs
headline.print_headline: From Dismal Chechnya, Women Turn to Bombs
